body:
{
  "title": "Anchor All-Hands Q1 2027",
  "compiled_truth": "The Q1 2027 all-hands was held on April 3rd at Anchor's main office, with the full core team present. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened with a recap of the quarter — revenue grew 38% QoQ, driven primarily by expansion deals from existing customers rather than net-new logos. Seren was candid about the fact that the sales pipeline had softened in February, attributing it partly to macro headwinds and partly to the team being stretched thin across too many proof-of-concept engagements. She emphasized that Q2 needs to be a \"focus quarter\" — fewer bets, deeper commitments.\n\n[Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) walked through the engineering OKR scorecard. The single-pass primitives hit a major milestone: deterministic throughput benchmarks now exceed the targets set during the Series A planning cycle. Dani noted that [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) deserves particular credit for refactoring the authentication layer, which had been a bottleneck since late 2026. Miro briefly demoed the new pipeline inspector tool, which drew genuine applause from the room. There was a small debate about whether to prioritize the SDK rewrite or the observability dashboard next — Dani deferred the decision to a dedicated planning session later in the week.\n\n[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) presented the product roadmap. The big theme for Q2 is \"enterprise readiness,\" which includes audit logging, granular access controls, and improved onboarding flows. Tal mentioned that two prospective customers in the financial services vertical have explicitly asked for SOC 2 compliance, and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) is now targeting a Type II report by end of year. [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) raised a concern about scope creep — she's already juggling customer success for seven accounts and worries the compliance workstream will pull enginering resources away from feature work.\n\n[Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) gave a brief update on hiring. Two backend engineering offers are out, one accepted. The search for a dedicated DevRel lead is still open. [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) shared early results from the content marketing push — blog traffic is up 4x since January, though conversion to demo requests remains low. [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) closed with a finance update: burn rate is stable, runway extends through Q1 2028 at current spend, but he flagged that the expanded hiring plan would shorten that to roughly nine months. Seren acknowledged this and said bridge financing conversations are already underway.\n\nThe mood was cautiously optimistic. The technology is working, customers are happy, but the team feels the weight of trying to do too much with too few people. Seren's closing remark — \"We're not in a sprint, we're in a relay\" — seemed to resonate.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2027-01-10** | Q1 OKRs finalized by [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei); engineering prioritizes authentication layer refactor.",
    "- **2027-01-28** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) begins work on the pipeline inspector tool for internal debugging and customer demos.",
    "- **2027-02-14** | Sales pipeline softens; two enterprise POCs stall due to procurement delays at prospect organizations.",
    "- **2027-02-26** | [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) launches new technical blog series on deterministic data infrastructure patterns; traffic begins climbing.",
    "- **2027-03-05** | [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) flags capacity concerns to [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura); customer success load now at seven active accounts.",
    "- **2027-03-18** | [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) delivers internal financial review; runway projection updated based on expanded hiring assumptions.",
    "- **2027-03-25** | First backend engineering offer accepted; second candidate still in negotiation.",
    "- **2027-04-03** | Q1 all-hands held at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) office; full team present for OKR review and Q2 roadmap discussion.",
    "- **2027-04-07** | Follow-up planning session scheduled to resolve SDK rewrite vs. observability dashboard prioritization.",
    "- **2027-04-15** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) targets posting the DevRel lead role on updated job board with revised compensation band."
  ]
}
